title: The Sunday slow morning
subtitle: A ritual for beginning one morning a week without a to-do list
excerpt: A ritual for Sunday mornings, or whichever morning works, that begins with no agenda, no phone, and no tasks for the first hour. A weekly anchor for a different quality of time.

practice.whenToUse: On a weekend morning when the week has been full and the impulse to immediately start the next list needs interrupting

content.text: A ritual for one morning a week. Sunday is the traditional choice, but the day doesn't matter. The principle is one hour without an agenda: no phone until the coffee is made, no planning what the day needs, no task-list opened. An hour of a different quality of time before the week's structure reasserts itself.
No phone until you're sitting with your drink
Get up. Make the coffee or tea. Do not pick up the phone until you are sitting somewhere comfortable with the drink in your hand. This single rule protects the first fifteen minutes.
Make the coffee as slowly as you'd make it if someone were watching. Grind by hand if you have it. Let the kettle cool a minute. The making is the first part of the ritual, not a preamble.
Sit somewhere other than your usual spot

The usual chair carries the usual associations. Sit somewhere slightly different: by a window you don't normally sit at, on the back step, in the garden if there is one. The slight change in vantage point produces a different quality of presence.
Stay without the plan for one full drink
Drink the whole thing without deciding what the day needs. Look out. Read something that isn't news. Watch what the light does. The hour doesn't have to be productive to be worthwhile.
